content.problemStatement:
You have a chocolate bar divided into NNN chunks, where each chunk has a sweetness value. You need to share this chocolate with KKK friends by making KKK cuts, resulting in K+1K+1K+1 pieces. Each piece must contain consecutive chunks. You'll eat the piece with the minimum total sweetness and give the rest to your friends. Your task is to find the maximum possible sweetness you can get for your piece.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains two integers NNN and KKK (1â‰¤Nâ‰¤104,1â‰¤Kâ‰¤Nâˆ’11 â‰¤ N â‰¤ 10^4, 1 â‰¤ K â‰¤ N-11â‰¤Nâ‰¤104,1â‰¤Kâ‰¤Nâˆ’1)
Second line contains NNN integers representing the sweetness of each chunk (1â‰¤sweetness[i]â‰¤1051 â‰¤ sweetness[i] â‰¤ 10^51â‰¤sweetness[i]â‰¤105)




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer representing the maximum possible minimum sweetness you can get.
constraints: SumofNacrossalltestcasesâ‰¤105Sum of N across all test cases \leq 10^5SumofNacrossalltestcasesâ‰¤105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Optimal division is [1,2,3], [4,5], [6], [7], [8], [9]. You get minimum piece of 6. 
Test Case 2: Only one way to divide into 9 pieces, minimum is 1. 
Test Case 3: Optimal division is [1,2,2], [1,2,2], [1,2,2]. You get minimum piece of 5.
